reasoning: The recent value for 1 + the 30-day average of battles in the Solomon Islands over the past 360 days was 1.000, meaning there were exactly 0 battles in the entire year preceding mid-October 2025. For the resolution criteria to be met, there must be more than 10 battles (i.e., 11 or more) in the 30 days preceding the resolution date (Jan 24, 2026). The Solomon Islands currently has no active armed conflict, and ACLED strictly defines 'Battles' as violent interactions between organized armed groups. Moving from an environment with 0 battles over a year to a highly intensive conflict with 11+ battles in a single 30-day window is exceptionally improbable. Therefore, the probability is set to the lower limit of 0.02.
